And so this promise is very real of like, you can actually build a real production app.

Yeah, I mean, obviously, it's like someone sees in their channel that like, hey, this is actually like working really, really well, we're open to it, and it's evolving all the time.

I think part of this, why we're putting top down like this, it's the entrepreneur narrative is, we only have six weeks, I guess, to kind of like build up proof points along that, along that narrative, and like vibe and positioning, and so a lot of this is informed by what we saw in the last, and the last

And the story we're going to be telling to investors in December of why is this interesting and why is this valuable, it's that actually there's an opportunity to build the new Shopify, that there's entrepreneurs that are out there.

And so, it's not that I'm not interested in, like, internal tools or, like, or, like, you know, other stories, but, like, I think in these next six weeks, the focus on, like, what's it take to get to entrepreneur-making app that's actually making significant revenue and the positioning of kind of across our, like, storytelling of that being consistent is almost more important, you got me?

But, yeah, of course, like, surface it, like, I don't know, if you see some insight, like, oh, wow, like, like, Korean game communities really, really love, like, like, building, like, small, like, game things, and we should take advantage of that and, and grow there, then by all means, surface it, and we'll change our mind as well.
It's just why, why I'm, why I'm emphasizing this is the point of emphasis.
Hey guys, Leo with IDoX Growth.
I have a question maybe for Marcus.
Does the revenue cut integration becomes live when the app is approved on the app store or that's separate?
Yes, but not discoverable to the agent.
So it will be installed so users could technically use it, but they probably won't get to like full success.
That's going to be an entirely new sprint in and of itself as well.
That's good question.
Got it.
Got it.
Just so people are aware, RevenueCat is the number one way to collect payments on mobile apps.
So we're launching and doing a partnership with them to actually, just in the same way we have Stripe on the web, like you can integrate Stripe by just saying like add payments to my app.
We're adding the ability to add RevenueCat and paywalls to your mobile apps.
So it's a big part of like this, wow, I built an app.
that actually is making significant money on the on the App Store, um, story.
Cool.
Anything else on narrative?
No?
Okay, I'll get some channels then and then I like open it up and I think we have like a couple of minutes left so let me just quickly talk about channels.
Um, really quickly this is a rough priority order of what we're prioritizing, um, on these vectors.

Um, we're standing up a bunch of them in parallel including some of the ones that are slightly longer term, um, but we need to lay the groundwork now like SEO and GEO and Reddit where it might drive traffic in the next like month, but like actually we just need to make sure we're always doing it.

Um, really quickly, X, Leo's leading that, um, we're doing two things here, both our own brand stuff and then Leo's actually driving UGC on X and doing a creator program for people to actually go viral on X and actually get our customers to talk about it.
LinkedIn, Kaylee and Sachin are running with this for our own content, um, we're working of the and we're
I'm finding someone for like UGC of our own customers.
TikTok and IG, there's a bunch of teams here that are focused on TikTok and IG.
When I kind of did the quick like stack rank of like what's like probably our number one bet is probably TikTok and IG because it can scale the fastest.
Zarya is running it on it from like our brand stuff.
Both Evo and Slideshift are working on UGC.
We're also working on bringing someone in-house to just constantly do like UGC partnerships.
On the influencer side of things, Cloud Kitchens is running an initial campaign.
And then we're also trying to spin up like internal influencer things.
But like TikTok as a big, as a really fast way to get the word out is definitely one of the priorities.
And then paired with TikTok and IG is actually paid ads.
So here on this call, Claire and Katie are running with Google.
And then we're bringing on Vince as well to help over time.
Meta Cloud Kitchen has done the initial thing for there.
Influencer Campaign, Vincent Team are also coming on board.
And then TikTok, we're not actually setting up TikTok ads, but we do want the UGC to help Google and Meta ads.
And we've kind of done a lot of work on attribution there.
And so I think those are the people, teams you need to know.
The X stuff, I think, could feed into the TikTok stuff, but I think Leo's kind of running it kind of independently.
Leo's also recruiting creators.
And so, like, that's a big part of, like, those might end up having TikTok.
Let me pull out a little bit on Vectors.
LinkedIn's a little bit separate.
On affiliates and, like, influencer partnerships, Vyame, who just spoke up, is running and chasing down lots of those.
We actually have an affiliate program on anything.
It gives, it's actually the best affiliate program in Vibe Coding, which is, like, one of the most lucrative spaces on the planet.
So it gives it.
About 30% of the revenue for anyone who subscribes from affiliate content.
And so we're, like, getting more and more affiliate creators.
But if you're working on TikTok or getting creators of any sort, I think pumping our affiliate deal is, like, a massive priority because it ends up, like, scaling faster.
And it's, like, another way to actually make money with anything is just teaching people how to use anything.
I'm trying to think of if there's anything else that's, like, really critical people know.
Okay, on Reddit, Lyra, I think, is on the call or is there as well.
Lyra is running with actually getting Reddit to blow up, kind of independently of the stuff we're doing for SEO and GEO.
On SEO and GEO, GrowthX and Trailblazer are both working on content and strategy.
I am right now going to do the end work for getting all that stuff coming up.
We'll soon have an engineer, like, replace me.
This is just landing pages, programmatic.
A actual, like, written content kind of at more scale than what we can write with the blog.
Zarya is doing a pretty big brand redesign.
MVP is working on consistent press kind of across the board.
We're not really yet prioritizing IG, YouTube, but we will.
And then final pieces as well, we're standing up a partnership program with Contra in early November, which lets them basically spin up experts that can help people with anything.
And then there's a ton of potential integrations that we're going to be marketing throughout, like, the next, like, handful of weeks.
One is RevenueCat, as Mark just kind of talked about.
Another one is potentially WAP and WAP Payments.
We're, like, working with a lot of these teams to get that up and running and, like, TBD if they'll land in the next six weeks.
But if they do, then it ends up driving a lot
Content Link, you can now actually build WAP apps or apps with like WAPs and payments, right?
And then Zarya is also working on a ton of kind of like in-person guerrilla marketing style things to get the word out in person in SF.
There's likely going to be, I don't know, other vectors we can push that out through, whether that's press or SEO or blog content or TikTok to some extent.
I'm trying to think those are like the big key vectors, like the big bets is that we can get TikTok really humming.
And so if you guys have ideas of how we can go even harder on TikTok sooner, faster, let us know.
X, really humming, and Leo's kind of leading this, paid ads, and then LinkedIn and affiliates.
These other vectors, but tell me how I'm wrong, guys.
Reddit, SEO,

YouTube, community, agency partnerships, well, like, integration partners, um, there's some of these that fall into the bucket of, like, it might just take a while for us to start getting indexed and ranked, um, and we should start that work now, and we're kind of excited for it, for in January and February and March as they start hitting, uh, there's others of it that has a little bit of, like, like, execution risk, for example, like, we're going to start building up a partnership program, we're going to start, like, like, we're going to do these, like, big partnerships, uh, with WAP and stuff, but the timelines are a little bit unclear, um, and so when we, like, look at our, like, overall stack rank, um, what we're really focused on to hit the 40, like, 40 million is all the channels it feels like we kind of have in our own control, uh, which is probably this whole top part, um, like, get the actual product to spread and then get the actual product out there with, like, on
tons of awareness.

We're almost at time.
Anyone else have anything else?
I got it, Jim.

Sounds good.
All right.
So guys, know there's a lot of content.

Bye.

Thank you.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Coordinate and align multiple go-to-market teams on Anything's aggressive growth strategy for the next 6 weeks.](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=154.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Anything aims to reach $40M ARR by December, driven by investor demo day opportunity](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=271.0)
  - [Focus on positioning Anything as the tool for entrepreneurs to build revenue-generating apps](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=1240.0)
  - [Prioritizing rapid scaling of multiple channels, especially TikTok/IG, paid ads, and X (Twitter)](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=2493.0)
  - Centralizing customer success stories and narratives in \#story Slack channel for cross-channel use

## Topics

### Company Context and Goals

  - [Anything launched August 7th, growing from 0 to $3M ARR](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=240.0)
  - [Closed $11M Series A in mid-September](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=240.0)
  - [Aiming for $40M ARR by December 4th (HFZero demo day)](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=271.0)
  - [Cash no longer a constraint; focus on rapid, efficient growth](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=379.0)
  - [Opportunity to potentially raise large Series B if execution is strong](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=328.0)

### Core Product and ICP

  - [Anything: AI-powered tool for building full-stack, mobile, and web apps without coding](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=970.0)
  - [Key differentiators: reliability, best mobile app builder, all-in-one solution, polished designs](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=985.0)
  - [Primary ICP: Non-technical, tech-adjacent internet entrepreneurs aiming to make money with apps](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=1483.0)
  - [Secondary ICPs include freelancers, agencies, and enterprise innovation teams](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=1568.0)

### Key Product Features and Updates

  - [Anything Max ($200/mo): Autonomous software engineer for QA and bug fixing](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=1878.0)
  - [Upcoming iOS app launch (pending Apple approval)](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=784.0)
  - [RevenueCat integration for mobile app monetization](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=2101.0)
  - [Potential partnerships with Wix and other platforms](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=919.0)

### Marketing Channels and Strategies

1.  TikTok/Instagram:
    
      - [Multiple teams working on UGC and influencer campaigns](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=2224.0)
      - [Seen as fastest potential scaling channel](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=2224.0)

2.  Paid Ads:
    
      - [Google Ads: Claire and Katie leading](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=2264.0)
      - [Meta Ads: Cloud Kitchen running initial campaign, Vince's team joining](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=2264.0)

3.  X (Twitter):
    
      - [Leo leading organic content and UGC creator program](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=2200.0)

4.  LinkedIn:
    
      - [Kaylee and Sachin managing organic content](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=2215.0)

5.  Affiliates and Partnerships:
    
      - [Guillaume managing affiliate program (30% revenue share)](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=2318.0)
      - [Contra partnership for expert marketplace launching in November](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=2421.0)

6.  SEO and Content:
    
      - [GrowthX and Trailblazer working on strategy](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=2375.0)
      - [Kaylee leading copywriting efforts](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=1371.0)

7.  PR and Brand:
    
      - [MVPR handling press relations](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=2406.0)
      - [Zaria leading brand redesign](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=2406.0)

8.  Community and In-Person:
    
      - [Zaria planning guerrilla marketing in San Francisco](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=2466.0)

### Content and Narrative Focus

  - [Positioning Anything as the tool for entrepreneurs to make money with apps](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=1240.0)
  - [Emphasizing reliability, mobile capabilities, and all-in-one solution](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=985.0)
  - [Showcasing customer success stories and revenue milestones](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=1333.0)
  - Centralizing narratives and stories in \#story Slack channel

## Next Steps

  - [Teams to review overall strategy and suggest optimizations for rapid scaling](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=2705.0)
  - Centralize customer success stories and share in \#story Slack channel
  - [Create and share updated public-facing stats document (users, ARR, apps built)](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=2630.0)
  - [Leverage upcoming CNBC interview content across multiple channels](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=2652.0)
  - [Continue aggressive channel testing and scaling, with focus on TikTok/IG, paid ads, and X](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=2493.0)
  - [Prepare for potential large partnerships (Wix, RevenueCat) and their marketing implications](https://fathom.video/share/Z5d7eDTd8r4D5ECp5HS2zGcR7EnWd9LZ?tab=summary&timestamp=2421.0)

action_items.description: Intro Evo (Dawson/Marty) to Vince/team re: Meta ads; then Evo shares top UGC for Meta/Spark
Add all GTM partners to #story; then post key narratives/launches/customer stories
Create and share Google Doc w/ latest users/ARR/apps; update regularly
